rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW O'REILLY VS. TREVOR MORGAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 22, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT TREVOR MORGAN, VONCHURCH SAN FRANCISCO INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the assault cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff does not sufficiently plead the reasonable apprehension (about to be touched) element of the cause of action. See Carlsen v. Koiyumaki (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 879, 890. Par. 15 of the amended complaint expressly alleges that plaintiff was "blind-side[d]" and did not see the genesis of the violent collision. The court overrules the demurrer to the battery cause of action. The court liberally reads the complaint per CCP 452. A factual issue remains regarding whether the battery occurred within the course and scope of employment and/or whether defendant VonChurch ratified the misconduct. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: ADAM P NOAH ET AL VS. ROBERT WAYNE DILLS INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA GREATER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 22, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT ROBERT DILLS' Motion To Set Aside Default. Denied. Defendant moves to set aside the default pursuant to CCP sec. 473(b) which requires a showing of "mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." Defendant states that he mistakenly "believed filing for bankruptcy for the Corporation would discharge all claims related to the construction contract, the subject of the complaint in this action." (Declaration of Robert Wayne Dills, par. 4.) The evidence submitted by Defendant does not establish an excusable mistake of law. First, Defendant never actually filed for bankruptcy on behalf of the corporation, as evidenced by the fact that Plaintiffs also took the default of the corporation. Defendant's declaration is silent on when he made the decision not to pursue the corporate bankruptcy, and why he did not move to set aside the default immediately after making that determination. Second, Defendant was served with the complaint in November 2014. Defendant's declaration is also silent on when he made the determination to file for bankruptcy on behalf of the corporation. Defendant states that he hired a bankruptcy attorney in April 2015, four months after his answer to the complaint was due. (Dills Decl., par. 5.) Defendant fails to explain how making a decision to file for bankruptcy four months after his answer was due excuses his failure to answer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: STEFAN SOBCZAK VS. SAKS & COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 22, 2015, Line 10, PLAINTIFF STEFAN SOBCZAK'S Motion To File First Amended Complaint. Denied. Plaintiff does not provide a plausible explanation about why leave to amend was not sought earlier. Plaintiff's contention that he did not learn about the facts giving rise to the proposed causes of action until his own deposition makes little sense. Additionally, Defendant submits evidence establishing that, at the very least, Plaintiff was aware of the facts in December 2014. (Declaration of Walter C. Pfeffer, Exs. Q-R.) Defendant has established that it will be unduly prejudiced by the delay, given that discovery has been completed and trial is set to commence in approximately two months. Further, Plaintiff's motion does not comply with the Rules of Court. CRC 3.1324 specifies what must be included in a declaration filed in support of a motion for leave to amend. The Declaration of Frank P. Sarro does not adequately address any of the requirements set forth in CRC 3.1324. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: MARY RYAN VS. SAFEWAY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant'S Amended Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 22, 2015, Line 2, Plaintiff Mary Ryan's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant's Amended Responses To Plaintiff's Requests For Production. Pro Tem Judge Sanford Kingsley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion was filed within 45 days of receipt of a verified supplemental response (CCP 2031.310(c)) and is properly before the Court. The motion is granted as follows: Requests Nos. 4 and 5 seek relevant and probative evidence but are overbroad as to time. Defendant is ordered to produce within 15 days all non-privileged responsive documents, to the extent such exist, for a period of three years prior to the date of the incident. Any responsive documents which are claimed to be privileged shall be logged on a privilege log which shall be provided to Plaintiff. Any claim of privilege for "Customer Accident Forms" (Frischer Declaration, Exh. F) is overruled as the form is to be completed and signed by the potential claimant and does not constitute a confidential attorney-client communication or a report prepared by an employee at the direction of counsel. Defendant may redact the names, addresses and phone numbers of customers other than Plaintiff listed on any such responsive documents. Request No. 6 seeks responsive and probative evidence. To the extent that there are any additional responsive documents beyond the Sweep policy already produced, they should be produced subject to the terms of the agreed upon protective order. Request No. 10 also seeks relevant and probative evidence, but is overbroad as to time and scope. Responsive non-privileged documents which pertain to the cleaning, maintenance and repair of the area of the store where Plaintiff was injured for a period of 30 days before the incident shall be produced. A privilege log shall also be provided for any privileged documents. Each party's respective requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sanfordkingsley@yahoo.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HEATHER MCCLOSKEY VS. PAYMENTWALL, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents And Motion For Protective Order To Stay Plaintiffs Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 22, 2015, Line 7, PLAINTIFF HEATHER MCCLOSKEY'S Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents And Motion For Protective Order To Stay Plaintiff's Deposition. Pro Tem Judge Sanford Kingsley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion for to compel responses to Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents is denied as moot. Defendants served a set of responses on June 19, 2015, the same day that Plaintiff appears to have initially served her motion to compel. The responses on their face appear not to be in compliance with the Code of Civil Procedure or drawn in good faith but that specific issue is not before the Court on the current motion (the current motion is brought under CCP section 2031.300 (failure to serve responses); a separate meet and confer process and a new motion under section 2031.310 are required to compel further responses). Plaintiff's motion for a protective order is denied. Under the Code of Civil Procedure there is no priority or staging of discovery other than a statutory right of defendants to serve a notice of deposition in the first 20 days after the defendant has been served with the complaint. Thus, Defendants' efforts to depose Plaintiff and Plaintiff's efforts to obtain documents from Defendants may proceed simultaneously and Plaintiff has no statutory right to require compliance with her document requests before submitting to deposition. The Court further notes that the first notice of deposition was served by Defendants on April 21, 2015 and Plaintiff's requests for production of documents were first served on April 24, 2015. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. This denial is without prejudice to a request for sanctions in connection with any motion under section 2031.310 to compel further responses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sanfordkingsley@yahoo.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
